Localization.Description: Password spraying via AD FS. Password spraying is a type of brute-force attack in which an attacker tries the most popular passwords sequentially for each user, which helps avoid blocking the accounts of the attacked users.

EventDescriptions.EventDescription: Successful password spraying via AD FS from host {src.host} on host {event_src.host}. Attackers obtained the passwords of the following users: {datafield9}. Number of failed login attempts: {count.subevents}.

Successful password spraying via AD FS from host {src.host} on host {event_src.host}. Attackers obtained the password of user {subject.account.name}. Number of failed login attempts: {count.subevents}.
Password spraying attempts via AD FS from host {src.host} on host {event_src.host}. Number of login attempts: {count.subevents}.
